D9 Biology L5 Evaluate Reference

Bacteriorhodopsin — Salt Lake Purple

Halobacterium uses a retinal-protein pump (BR) instead of chlorophyll; the protein-bound retinal absorbs 570 nm green-yellow → reflects purple — same kind of pigment as in your eye, very different host

Halobacterium salinarum (now Halobacterium NRC-1) lives in salt-saturated brine. It runs an ATP-generating proton pump driven by light, not the electron transport of chlorophyll. The chromophore is retinal (the same molecule in your rhodopsin), covalently bound to lysine via a Schiff base. The protein cavity shifts retinal's λ_max to 570 nm — the famous Halobacterium purple.

Toggle "soluble vs membrane-embedded" retinal to see the Schiff-base + protein environment shift λ_max from ~440 nm yellow to 570 nm green-absorbing.
